---
title: "Project Data Vis&Comm"
author: "Annika Rodriguez"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(highcharter)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)

```

```{r}
youtube <- read.csv("~/youtube_clean.csv")
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

youtube <- youtube |> 
  mutate(title_length = nchar(title))

ggplot(youtube, aes(x = title_length)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "red", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max(youtube$title_length), by = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of YouTube Trending Video Title Lengths",
    x = "Title Length (Number of Characters)",
    y = "Number of Trending Videos") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

```

```{r}
hour_labels_12 <- format(strptime(0:23, "%H"), "%I %p")

heatmap_hour_engagement <- youtube |>
  mutate(
    publish_hour = as.numeric(publish_hour),
    engagement_rate = (likes + comment_count) / views) |>
  group_by(publish_hour) |>
  summarize(
    Avg_engagement = mean(engagement_rate, na.rm = TRUE) * 1000,
    .groups = "drop")

red_colors <- c("#FFEBEE", "#FFCDD2", "#EF9A9A", "#E57373", "#EF5350", "#D32F2F", "#B71C1C")
explicit_stops <- color_stops(colors = red_colors)

heatmap_hour_engagement |> 
  hchart(
    type = "heatmap",
    hcaes(
      x = publish_hour,
      y = 1,
      value = Avg_engagement)) |>
  
  hc_add_theme(hc_theme_538()) |>
  
  hc_colorAxis(
    stops = explicit_stops,
    labels = list(format = "{value}%")) |>
  
  hc_title(text = "🔥 Average Engagement Rate by Publish Hour") |>
  
  hc_xAxis(
    title = list(text = "Publish Hour (12-hour clock)"),
    categories = hour_labels_12) |>
  
  hc_yAxis(
    title = list(text = ""),
    labels = list(enabled = FALSE),
    tickWidth = 0) |>
  
  hc_tooltip(
    pointFormat = "<b>Publish Hour:</b> {point.category}<br>
                   <b>Avg Engagement:</b> {point.value:.2f}%")

```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(highcharter)


youtube <- youtube |>
  mutate(
    publish_wday = factor(
      publish_wday,
      levels = c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun")))

weekday_counts <- youtube |>
  group_by(publish_wday) |>
  summarize(video_count = n(), .groups = "drop")

weekday_counts <- weekday_counts |>
  mutate(color = "#D32F2F") 

highchart() |>
  hc_chart(type = "column") |>
  
  hc_add_series(
    data = list_parse(
      weekday_counts |>
        select(name = publish_wday, y = video_count, color)),
    name = "Trending Videos",
    colorByPoint = TRUE) |>
  
  hc_colors(weekday_counts$color) |>
  
  hc_title(
    text = "📅 Trending Video Publishing Patterns by Weekday",
    style = list(fontWeight = "bold", fontSize = "20px")) |>
  
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Which days have the strongest visibility signal?",
    style = list(fontSize = "13px", color = "grey40")) |>
  
  hc_xAxis(
    categories = weekday_counts$publish_wday,
    title = list(text = NULL),
    labels = list(rotation = 0)) |>
  
  hc_yAxis(
    title = list(text = "Number of Trending Videos"),
    labels = list(format = "{value}")) |>
  
  hc_add_theme(hc_theme_google()) |>
  
  hc_plotOptions(
    column = list(
      dataLabels = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        format = "<b>{point.y}</b>",
        style = list(fontSize = "13px")),
      borderRadius = 3)) |>
  
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE)
```
